channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Africa Forward Summit in Kenya: France offers ‘third way’ in Africa’s AI race
item.description: This Monday, French President Emmanuel Macron is in Nairobi, where he is co-hosting the Africa Forward Summit with his Kenyan counterpart William Ruto – a summit focused on trade, investment and tech. Kenya is hoping to scale up its ability to meet Africa’s growing artificial intelligence needs. For France, this summit is an opportunity to offer a third way between US and Chinese tech, as FRANCE 24's team on the ground reports.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: White House press gala shooting suspect pleads not guilty to attempted assassination of Donald Trump
item.description: A California man accused of attempting to assassinate US President Donald Trump at last month's White House Correspondents' Dinner pleaded not guilty to all charges on Monday. Prosecutors allege Cole Allen fired ⁠a shotgun at a US Secret Service agent and stormed a security checkpoint in a foiled attack in April.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Ukraine and Russia fight on despite US-mediated ceasefire
item.description: Russia ​and Ukraine on Monday reported fighting along their long front line despite a US-mediated ceasefire, and each accused the other of launching drone and artillery strikes. Ukraine and Russia agreed on Friday to a ​ceasefire from ‌May 9 to May 11 as part of a US-led push ⁠for peace under President Donald Trump after more than four years of war following Russia's full-scale invasion in 2022. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hantavirus: France enforces strict measures after citizen tests positive
item.description: One person in France has tested positive for Hantavirus. This comes after five people from the cruise ship were repatriated on Sunday after the vessel docked in the Spanish port of Tenerife. According to the French minister the patient's condition worsened overnight.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hantavirus: French PM Lecornu holds talks with medical advisers, ministers
item.description: A French passenger who was on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak has tested positive for the virus and her condition is deteriorating, French authorities said. France's Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu will hold a meeting on the hantavirus crisis on Monday. FRANCE 24's James André reports from Paris, France.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Changing geopolitics, deals and new partnerships on the menu at Africa-France summit
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron ​and more than 30 African leaders kicked off a summit in Kenya on Monday aimed at diversifying Paris' partnerships on the continent and clinching new investment deals. The Africa Forward Summit is the first France has organised in an ​English-speaking nation ‌since it began holding such events in the 1970s and follows a series of ⁠setbacks in former colonies in West Africa that have moved to reduce French influence.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Witch Post's spellbinding EP 'Butterfly' and goodbye to Charli XCX's 'Brat Summer'
item.description: In this edition of our arts24 music show, Jennifer Ben Brahim chats with Scottish-American duo Witch Post. Despite living more than 4,000 miles apart, Alaska Reid and Dylan Fraser crossed geographical barriers to form this band, united by a shared love of indie-rock, songwriting and folklore from their respective homelands. They are on tour with their sophomore EP "Butterfly", a record steeped in fantasy and the supernatural.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer BEN BRAHIM

item.title: Boycotters Spain, Ireland, Slovenia will not show Eurovision
item.description: The public broadcasters for Spain, Ireland and Slovenia said Monday they will not show the 70th anniversary Eurovision Song Contest this week, as they boycott the TV extravaganza over Israel's participation.

item.title: EU approves new sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
item.description: The European Union on Monday approved new sanctions on Israeli settlers "guilty of supporting the extremist and violent colonisation of the West Bank", French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot said on social media. Israel called the sanctions “arbitrary and political” and vowed to stand '”for the right of Jews” to settle in the West Bank.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: 'I will make order': Macorn calls for silence at Africa summit
item.description: The French president is currently in #Nairobi for the Africa Forward summit, a gathering meant to showcase France’s new policy for the continent. It is the first time the summit is held in an English-speaking country , and is attended by more than 30 presidents, deputy presidents and prime ministers from across Africa. It also comes at a time where #France has undergone a series of setbacks in former West African colonies aiming to reduce its influence.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hantavirus ship evacuation nears completion as 94 flown home
item.description: A complex day-long operation to repatriate occupants of a cruise ship struck by a deadly hantavirus outbreak neared completion late Sunday after 94 people of various nationalities were flown home from Spain's Canary Islands. Three passengers from the MV Hondius -- a Dutch husband and wife and a German woman -- have died, while others have fallen sick with the rare disease, which usually spreads among rodents. No vaccines or specific treatments exist for hantavirus, which is endemic in Argentina, where the ship departed in April.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: UK's Starmer promises to be bolder to try to rescue his job
item.description: Prime Minister ​Keir Starmer promised to be bolder to turn around Britain's fortunes, making an impassioned plea to both his Labour Party and voters on Monday to stick with him and avoid a leadership contest he said would only bring chaos. Speaking at a community centre in London, Starmer all but admitted he had been too timid in tackling the myriad of problems besetting Britain since he won a ​large majority in 2024, and ‌said he took responsibility for one of the worst defeats for Labour in last week's elections.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran-US ceasefire grows shaky after Trump rejects Tehran's latest proposal
item.description: Iran and the United States were at an impasse again Monday over how to end their war while their ceasefire grew increasingly shaky, with the two sides exchanging fire in recent days, ships and Gulf states being targeted, and fighting flaring between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Angela Diffley tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: More confirmed hantavirus cases: The situation in France
item.description: A French ​passenger who was on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak has tested positive for ​the ‌virus and her condition ⁠is deteriorating, French Health Minister Stephanie Rist said on ‌Monday. The passenger was among five French ⁠people who were on the ship. The four other passengers tested negative. So far French authorities have traced 22 contact cases. FRANCE 24's Environment Editor Valerie Dekimpe tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Closing arguments in Sarkozy trial: Ex-French pdt appealing verdict in Libya finance case
item.description: In France, nearly two months after the start of the appeal trial concerning allegations of Libyan financing of Nicolas Sarkozy's 2007 presidential campaign, the prosecution presented its closing arguments on Monday. FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizot reports.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Starmer vows to prove 'doubters' wrong, bring UK closer to the EU to rescue his job
item.description: Embattled British Prime Minister Keir Starmer came out fighting Monday, vowing to prove his "doubters" wrong as he sought to quell a growing threat to his leadership following disastrous election results. Starmer pledged his ruling Labour party would be "better" and bolder as he tried to convince angry and restless lawmakers to back him in a crunch speech designed to reset his flagging premiership. FRANCE 24's Bénédicte Paviot reports from London.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: ‘It’s organised intimidation’: New wave of anti-migrant violence sweeps South Africa
item.description: Videos posted online document the new wave of deadly anti-migrant violence that has been sweeping South Africa since late March 2026, killing at least seven people. Organised anti-migrant movements are inflaming tensions, which are exacerbated by widespread unemployment and persistent inequality.
item.dc:creator: The FRANCE 24 Observers

item.title: Trump rejects Iran's response to US peace proposal as 'unacceptable'
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday branded Iran's terms for ending the Middle East war "totally unacceptable," raising the likelihood of renewed conflict after weeks of negotiations. Iran had responded to Washington's latest peace proposal earlier in the day, while warning it would not hold back from retaliating against any new US strikes or permit more foreign warships in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Suspected hantavirus case triggers emergency deployment to isolated island
item.description: As Americans are evacuated from the Dutch-flagged cruise ship linked to the hantavirus outbreak, two passengers have now tested positive. But in the South Atlantic, a scene from a Hollywood thriller: paratroopers dropping onto one of the world’s most remote inhabited islands with emergency medical supplies after a suspected case. A British national is now in a stable condition while isolating.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: PM Starmer clings on to power as Labour rivals make moves
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – May 11, 2026: First, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is under pressure following the Labour Party's disastrous results in the local elections. Next, the Guardian features an exclusive extract from Iranian Nobel Prize winner Narges Mohammadi's diaries. Meanwhile, the situation in Cuba is on Libération's front page. Finally, Spanish sports papers celebrate Barcelona as the new La Liga champions.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak: US, French nationals test positive after evacuating ship
item.description: An American citizen and a French woman evacuated from the cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak have tested positive, officials said, as the repatriation operation continued on Monday. Following the positive test results, Spain defended the rigour of its sanitary measures during the complex evacuation Sunday of 94 people of 19 different nationalities from the MV Hondius, which is moored in the Canary Islands. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin reports from Bichat Hospital in central Paris.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran Nobel winner Narges Mohammadi released on bail for medical treatment
item.description: Iranian authorities on Sunday released Nobel peace prize winner Narges Mohammadi on bail following growing alarm over her health and she has already been transferred to Tehran for medical treatment, her supporters said. They had last week warned that Mohammadi, who won the 2023 prize in recognition of her decades of campaigning for human rights in Iran, was at risk of dying on prison after suffer two suspected heart attacks behind bars in Zanjan.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Russia, China and Iran: What do Beijing and Moscow want to gain from conflict?
item.description: Iran and the United States reached an impasse again Monday over how to end their war while their ceasefire grew increasingly shaky. As the situation in the Middle East keeps the world on edge, many regional observers say Iran’s resilience is thanks in part to the lifeline extended by two key allies: Russia and China. Both countries, at least publicly, say they seek de-escalation. But Western observers say they are providing crucial support to Teheran to help keep it in the fight. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: French passenger from hantavirus ship tests positive
item.description: A French woman repatriated from a cruise ship struck by hantavirus has tested positive with the rare disease, France's health minister said Monday, in the country's first case since the outbreak. The woman, one of five French passengers flown back from the MV Hondius and placed in isolation in Paris, started to feel very unwell on Sunday night and "tests came back positive", Health Minister Stephanie Rist told the radio station France Inter. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin reports from Paris, France.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
